preview_text:
The model favors Algeria at 44%, but rates Jordan as a credible challenger at 26%. The underdog thus retains genuine winning equity despite a 18-point deficit in headline probability.

The 31% draw probability suggests a competitive, closely matched encounter where neither side is heavily favored to impose dominance. The win spread is moderate rather than pronounced—Algeria holds a clear but not overwhelming edge. A stalemate remains a plausible outcome, indicating the teams are expected to contest the match evenly through regulation.

Model and market diverge meaningfully. The sportsbook consensus skews toward Algeria at 62%, a gap of 18 percentage points above the model, while simultaneously pricing Jordan at 15%—11 points lower than the model's 26%. Polymarket pricing aligns closely with sportsbook sentiment, offering Algeria 66% and Jordan just 14%. The dashboard does not adjudicate which assessment better reflects true match probability; the divergence reflects different weightings of available information.
